entry.title: SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition - The “Trust Layer” for Agentic AI
entry.content:
For the last year, the world has been captivated by AI’s ability to answer. The next era belongs to AI that can act.

Agentic AI promises something bigger than copilots and chat interfaces: a digital workforce that can sense what’s happening, reason over context, and initiate the next best action across finance, supply chain, HR, procurement, and customer operations. But here’s the reality most enterprises discover quickly: the leap from clever prototype to production-grade agent isn’t a model problem — it’s a trust problem.

Trust is earned in the connective tissue of the enterprise: identity, governance, reliable integration, observability, and controlled execution across SAP and non‑SAP systems. That’s exactly why we’re excited to announce SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition — designed to help organizations move from AI experimentation to agentic outcomes by providing an integration foundation where agents can operate safely, securely, and at scale.

From “AI that knows” to “AI that does”: why integration becomes strategic

Agentic AI doesn’t live in a single application. It lives between applications — where approvals happen, where exceptions are resolved, where events trigger downstream actions, where APIs enforce guardrails, and where process continuity must be protected.

This is the moment when integration becomes more than plumbing. It becomes the execution fabric for AI.

SAP’s vision for Integration Suite is explicit: unify your enterprise with secure, AI‑ready integration and the governance required to scale agentic AI across the business — not as an add-on, but as an enterprise capability.

What’s new in enhanced edition — and why it’s built for agentic reality

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition provides access to newly released AI capabilities that focus on two things agentic AI needs most:

1) Speed to value: AI that accelerates how integrations are built

Enhanced edition includes AI features aimed at boosting developer productivity, including:

  • Text‑to‑Flow generation — build integration flows using a natural language interface.
  • Script Optimization — analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript scripts inside iFlows.

In an agentic world, integration teams must iterate faster — because agents will continuously evolve how they use tools, call APIs, and respond to events. These capabilities are about compressing the distance between an idea and a governed, deployable integration asset.

2) Operational trust: AI that helps integrations run with confidence

Agents introduce new scale and new patterns — and that means you need sharper operational insight. Enhanced edition includes AI-driven operations efficiency features such as:

  • API Anomaly Detection — AI-driven observability that detects unusual behavior and flags issues early.
  • API Traffic Prediction — identify trends and predict upcoming API call volumes to support smarter planning and load management.

This is what makes the positioning “agentic enabler” real: it’s not just that AI helps you build integrations — it helps you run the execution fabric with the resilience and foresight required for autonomous action.

More than AI features: the full suite that agents can safely act through

Enhanced edition also includes the broader platform capabilities needed to operationalize agentic scenarios end-to-end, including:

  • The full suite of integration capabilities (application integration, B2B integration, API management, edge integration) and broad connectivity.
  • Integration Suite, advanced event mesh for event-driven architecture use cases.
  • SAP Document AI, plus Alert Notification Service and Transport Management Service to support intelligent automation patterns and governed lifecycle management.

In other words: enhanced edition is designed to be a practical on‑ramp to a future where AI agents don’t just inform decisions — they execute decisions across the enterprise, under control.

How customers can move to enhanced edition (designed to be seamless)

Many customers already run SAP Integration Suite today — and moving to enhanced edition is intended to be an in-place plan upgrade rather than a disruptive reimplementation.

We provide documented upgrade guidance for updating an existing tenant to a higher service plan (with the note that it’s a commercial metadata update and is irreversible once applied). See official documentation on help.sap.com.
Additionally, our community guidance on tenant upgrades has been updated to include upgrade paths to the newly released enhanced_edition. See SAP Note 2903776.

For customers modernizing from older integration platforms, SAP also provides structured guidance and tools to support transition journeys to SAP Integration Suite. See migration factory page on sap.com.

Commercial options: choose the model that matches your AI journey

Agentic adoption is rarely linear — which is why commercial flexibility matters.

SAP BTP supports two commercial models:

  • Subscription-based: fixed cost for subscribed services.
  • Consumption-based: flexibility to use eligible services as business needs evolve, available via models such as SAP BTP Enterprise Agreement / Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement and Pay‑As‑You‑Go for SAP BTP.

And if your organization wants a low-friction entry point, choose Integration Suite free tier and a path to upgrade to a paid service plan.

You can find out more details on the different commercial models on SAP Discovery Centre.

A note on AI feature availability and rollout

The availability of AI features depend on the availability of SAP AI Core and the specific LLM model and corresponding version on that data centre. If you notice that AI features are not available in the DC of your choice, please get in touch with us via the comments section regarding your requirement.

For detailed information on the availability of different AI features in specific DCs, please refer to this SAP Note: https://me.sap.com/notes/3463620

The bigger picture: building an enterprise where AI can safely act

The most important shift isn’t “AI inside an app.” It’s AI as a participant in end-to-end business execution.

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition is built for that shift — providing the integration fabric where autonomous agents can access governed tools, respond to events, operate across hybrid landscapes, and execute actions with observability and control.

If your organization is serious about moving from AI inspiration to agentic transformation, the most strategic question isn’t “Which model should we use?” It is: Do we have an integration foundation strong enough for AI to act — at enterprise scale?

Frequently Asked Questions on Enhanced Edition

What are the commercial transition options to Enhanced Edition?

If you are a consumption-based (BTPEA/CPEA) Integration Suite customer, you already have access to Enhanced Edition. Just upgrade your current tenants to Enhanced Edition via our seamless upgrade process. If you are a subscription customer, please get in touch with your customer success partner or account executive to understand the best path to obtain Enhanced Edition entitlements.

If you are a customer using one of our older standalone service licenses, please reach out to us via your customer success partner or account executive as well for more details on how to obtain Enhanced Edition entitlements.

What is the technical upgrade/downgrade options to/from Enhanced Edition?

Technically, we support a seamless upgrade from any of the current Integration Suite plan to the Enhanced Edition. More details here: upgrade process & upgrade paths

We do not support downgrading Enhanced Edition tenants to any of the other Integration Suite editions.

Can we have a mix of Standard/Premium tenant with Enhanced Edition in the same landscape?

We do not recommend this since it is critical to have feature parity across the different landscape tiers.

I don’t have AI units; can I still use AI features in Enhanced Edition?

Yes. Currently, we are offering AI features as a promotion. Soon, we will start charging the AI features via AI units. Please stay tuned to this space as more details will follow in the next weeks on this topic.

What happens if I reach the fair usage limit for AI features in Enhanced Edition?

You will have to wait for the next calendar month to begin when your usage quota gets reset. This is a limitation only till we launch metering AI feature usage via AI units, after which you can buy more quota and use AI features based on your requirement.

Is MCP already available on Enhanced Edition if I am a beta customer?

Not yet; it will be launched soon. Please refer to our roadmap for the updated timelines.

How can I purchase additional quota for the included services like Document AI, TMS, ANS, etc. in Enhanced Edition?

If you are on a consumption-based (BTPEA/CPEA) license or a hybrid (subscription+consumption) license, you already have access to the services via your credits and can use them, once you it the limits in Enhanced Edition.

If you are a subscription-only customer, please get in touch with your customer success manager or account executive to buy more quota.

Can I upgrade the AEM broker to a larger size? What happens to the existing entitlement if I upgrade?

Yes, the broker can be upgraded to a bigger size. You will either be charged on your consumption license or will have to procure a subscription license for the bigger broker of your choice before the upgrade process. 

The entitlement will continue to remain, and you can provision another standard-100 broker if you wish.

Is there a dedicated SKU for a non-production tenant of Enhanced Edition?

No. We only have one SKU for Enhanced Edition and it can be used in both production and non-production modes.

Is there a dedicated TDD SKU for Enhanced Edition?

This is currently work in progress and will be launched in the near future. Please stay tuned to this space for more information.

entry.title: Q1/2026 Product Highlights - SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Welcome to another quarterly recap of the latest released features in SAP Integration Suite! The new capabilities in Q1/2026 focus on better operations and smoother eventing for integration practitioners who build, run, and troubleshoot integrations every day.  Enjoy the highlights and check the linked docs and demos for how-to details and the road map for the full list.

Enhanced Edition is here

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition helps move AI and agentic AI from pilots to production by providing governed, real-time connectivity, low-code developer tools, and AI-driven operations (Text-to-Flow, Script Optimization, API Anomaly Detection, API Traffic Prediction, plus connectors, event mesh, Document AI, alerting, and transport management). It reduces integration debt, enables secure agent actions across cloud and on-prem systems, and creates reusable building blocks for faster, repeatable AI value.

Accelerate your AI initiatives and make them enterprise-ready, get more details in the SAP Discovery Center.

Event-driven Integration

Discovery and publication of advanced event mesh events in the Developer Hub

Content administrators can discover and publish Advanced Event Mesh events directly in the Developer Hub, enabling events to be exposed as event products and making it easier for application developers to search and find relevant events. To use it, you create a destination to connect SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh to the Developer Hub; once connected, the business system appears in the Developer Hub for discovery and publication.

For more detailed instructions, check the documentation: Discover and Publish Events from Advanced Event Mesh in Developer Hub.

 

Migration support from SAP Event Mesh to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This update provides technical and process-oriented support to help customers migrate from SAP Event Mesh (default plan) to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, enabling organizations to move their event-driven architectures to a more powerful, feature-rich platform. The guide targets customers already using the SAP Event Mesh default plan and outlines steps to expand their event-based architecture. Read the guide: Migration From SAP Event Mesh to SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh.

Process Integration

Valkey support for Edge Integration Cell

The Edge Integration Cell capability in SAP Integration Suite now supports Valkey as an alternative datastore option alongside Redis and HANA DB. This option is available as selectable installation supporting versions 8.0-8.2 and allows administrators to choose Valkey for deployments helping reduce total cost of ownership.

For the steps and prerequesites, see: Deploy the Edge Integration Cell Solution

Monitor Message Processing on SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM

Users can monitor message processing from Edge Integration Cell using a push-based approach to send logs to SAP Focused Run (FRUN) and SAP Cloud ALM (CALM). This enables centralized monitoring of multiple Edge Integration Cell runtimes within a single application, lets you configure alerts based on message processing log status, and supports monitoring of Edge Integration Cell message processing logs in SAP Focused Run even during offline scenarios.

See Monitor Message Processing on SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM for more information.

Message queue management: extension and filtering of scenario details in the message queue monitor

The message queue monitor now displays scenario-specific attributes, such as Sender, Receiver, Application Message Type, Application Message ID, and Correlation ID, which can be enabled in the Messages table settings and used as filter criteria.

This feature lets you narrow messages more efficiently and quickly identify problematic entries with immediate context, reducing mean time to resolution by allowing direct filtering to relevant messages, enabling more precise troubleshooting and root-cause analysis, cutting operational noise by surfacing only the messages that matter, improving auditing and compliance with visible searchable metadata, and simplifying collaboration and handover between operations, support, and development teams. Get started: Managing Messages Stored in Queues

Support for parameterized mappings for the adapter type

The import options from SAP Process Orchestration into SAP Integration Suite - Cloud Integration have been extended to support parameters in message mappings for the adapter category, enabling you to import PI/PO message mappings that include adapter-type parameters; migration tooling and standalone message mapping import now accept mapping objects with the Adapter Type parameter category of type Import, which preserves adapter-specific parameterization, reduces manual adjustments and rework during migration, improves fidelity of migrated mappings, and accelerates onboarding and troubleshooting by maintaining runtime behavior tied to adapter parameters. See documentation: Import a Message Mapping from Enterprise Services Repository (ESR)

Connectivity Updates

Customer-managed cloud connectors

The Edge Integration Cell runtime now supports using a customer-managed external SAP Cloud Connector instead of the built-in, SAP-managed connector, allowing organizations to deploy the connector in a network zone that best fits their infrastructure and security requirements. This feature adds deployment flexibility, improves security controls, enables additional traffic inspection capabilities, and helps meet infrastructure and compliance needs. See configuration steps: Configure an External Cloud Connector for Edge Integration Cell

Additionally, users can now add and initialize an Edge Node with an external Cloud Connector through Edge Lifecycle Management. See how: Initializing an Edge Node with External Cloud Connector

Latest adapters

The list of released adapter in Q1/2026 includes:

  • BambooHR adapter: Connect to BambooHR to access APIs related to employee, organizational, time off data etc.
  • Zendesk adapter: Offers a comprehensive set of Ticketing CRUD operations for various entities such as Tickets, Attachments, Requests, Tags, etc. The adapter is designed to receive and process data from an integration flow in SAP Integration Suite and facilitate communication with Zendesk application (external) using REST-based APIs.
  • Microsoft 365 Outlook adapter: Enables seamless integration between Microsoft Outlook and SAP Integration Suite by improving communication and automating workflows between the two platforms. Available as a Sender and Receiver adapter. 🎥 Watch the demo. 
  • OFTP2 adapter: Use the Odette File Transfer Protocol (OFTP) adapter to exchange EDI and business files with partners on your Edge Integration Cell.
  • NFS File adapter: Enables connectivity to NFS server facilitating read and write operations for files on your Edge Integration Cell.  🎥 Watch the demo.

Updated data center availability

The new data center availability options for SAP Integration Suite include: Australia (Sydney) on SAP infrastructure, Japan (Tokyo) and USA on an SAP-hosted infrastructure, and the United Kingdom through Microsoft Azure.

---

Learn more

Want a sneak peek into what else is in store for SAP Integration Suite in 2026? Watch this walkthrough:

 What are the key innovation you're looking forward to in 2026? Let me know in the comments!

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration (CI) for SAP IBP HPA integration: A real-world implementation case study
entry.content:
In one of the previous blog post, SAP Cloud Integration was introduced as new integration option for SAP IBP. Subsequent blog posts covered technical topics for creating integration flows based on SAP delivered packages and flows for different integration scenarios. These blog posts in addition to highlighting why organizations should consider SAP CI as part of their IBP integration strategy, covered fundamental set-up and configuration steps for using it for integrating SAP IBP with S/4 Hana Cloud.

In this blog, we explore a real-world example of an organization using Cloud Integration to overcome complex integration challenges for their IBP HPA implementation. We share details of challenges, key architectural decisions, and lessons learned from this complex supply chain planning implementation. This blog highlights a proven integration approach that offers valuable learnings for organizations navigating similar challenges.

Integration challenges: Distributed landscape

The organization in focus like other large manufacturing organizations operates an extensive global supply chain. Their landscape includes:

  • Multiple ERP system distributed globally
  • Large product portfolio with complex supply chain spread across locations using different systems

The key challenge was to integrate data from multiple SAP and Non-SAP systems. The integration approach needs to be flexible enough to support multiple source system integration with SAP IBP.

Key decision: Cloud Integration or Real Time Integration

One of the most critical decisions in any IBP implementation is to choose the right integration tool for IBP.  From release 2508 onwards, RTI and SAP CI are the two integration tools available to integrate SAP IBP HPA with SAP S4 Hana private cloud.

 

The sample customer’s implementation team made an early decision to go with a standalone CI integration approach for IBP. Some of the drivers for this decision were:

  • Cloud Integration works well with all SAP and non-SAP ERP systems, compared to RTI which is restricted only to S/4HANA Private edition
  • Readily available Cloud Integration expertise within the organization
  • Operational planning is carried out in ERP system, ruling out need for Order based planning in IBP
  • Business followed monthly planning cycle, with delta data load planned daily

Additionally, the availability of prepackaged reusable assets for SAP Cloud Integration supports accelerated implementation and reduces time to value significantly. It provides comprehensive connectivity options for diverse landscape, and it being under active development from SAP keeps your integration architecture future ready. Its web-based centralized monitoring and error handling capabilities help in reducing the overall maintenance cost. Because of these inherent advantages of SAP CI, the implementation team decided to go with standalone SAP CI integration architecture.

As real-time data sync capability was not needed, RTI didn’t offer any additional business value in comparison to CI in this scenario. However, depending on your business needs, Operational planning (OBP) in IBP and real time sync capabilities could be essential, in that case CI can be used in conjunction with RTI. As per SAP guidelines, integration architecture can utilize both the integration technologies following way:

  • RTI used for integrating master data and order data from S/4 Hana Private edition
  • SAP Cloud Integration (CI) used for all other integration scenarios such as Key Figures or other non-standard objects.

Following SAP help portal page helps you find right integration tool for your business needs: Finding the Right Integration Tool | SAP Help Portal

Integration design philosophy: keep interfaces “Lean” and “Scalable”

An important contributing factor to the successful implementation of CI for SAP IBP was to keep interfaces simple. The customer's implementation team followed a clear guiding principle that Cloud Integration (CI) should be used only for transporting data from one system to another. It should not be used for deriving any business logic. Cloud Integration (CI) is best used for:

  • Data transportation
  • Data format transformation and technical validation
  • Security handling

Business rules, process logic, and calculations were handled in the source or target system. This approach of preprocessing data in source or target system, made sure CI flows are simple, maintainable and reusable.

Preprocessing of data in source system:

To keep the core and IBP Add-on clean on the S/4 Hana, the implementation team decided to use extended CDS views for data preprocessing in S/4 Hana. Cloud integration flows read data directly from CDS views, avoiding modification of extractors and therefore sticking to clean core best practices.

How it works: Multi layered CDS views were created for preprocessing data in S/4 Hana:

  1. Reuse layer CDS views: Built on IBP add-on staging tables and raw tables (for additional data), this layer provided filtered data. As these CDS view also included data from raw tables, any custom data fields not available in IBP add-on staging tables were captured in these CDS views
  2. Consumption layer CDS views: This is primarily used for renaming S/4 fields to IBP fields. Reuse layer CDS views served as input for these CDS views. CI flows read data directly from these CDS views.

Above approach along with Change Data Capture (CDC) capability for CDS extraction allows delta loads for master data and S/4 Hana execution transaction data.

Reusable integration flows in CI:

SAP CI provides prepackaged reusable integration flows, that can be used to integrate all master data and transaction data types with minimal configuration changes. For the sample customer, data integration is executed by running a single prepackaged reusable CI iflow. One reusable integration flow handles all integration scenarios – all master data and key figures, with the help of flexible parameters. These parameters define:

  • Source CDS view to read in S/4 Hana
  • Target Master data and Key figures in IBP
  • Transfer mode: delta or full load

By sticking to preprocessing within the source system using CDS views and integrating using reusable flow, solution is flexible to accommodate future requirements. Any future business requirements can be handled by extending CDS views in the source system, avoiding any changes in the integration layer.

Integration with non-SAP system:

To integrate non-SAP ERP systems, data from multiple ERP systems is first integrated into a Data warehousing system. In Data warehousing system similar CDS view dependent process is used to preprocess data and then a reusable CI flow is used to pull data into IBP. This unified approach means consistent data structure regardless of the source system type.

Back integration to S4 Hana system:

IBP add-on staging tables enable a fire and forget approach for integrating planning output data from IBP to S/4 Hana. CI delivers data to S/4 IBP add-on tables and S/4 process them independently.  

With this lean integration approach, figure below shows high level data flow between different systems:

 

 Key takeaways: Some of the key learnings from this successful implementation of CI integration are:

  • Evaluating business requirements to select the right integration tool for your implementation. Depending on specific scenarios, CI only integration strategy can be effective.
  • Keep integration lean and focus on data transport only. Push transformation and business logic in source and target systems
  • Availability of prepackaged integration flow helps in accelerated implementation, and results in scalable and low maintenance integration solution

entry.title: Cloud Integration with Translation Hub
entry.content:
Introduction:

In this blog post, I will demonstrate how to connect Cloud Integration with Translation Hub. As a use case, consider a scenario where a sender system submits an order request. If the order creation fails, the system expects the error message to be returned in both English and Japanese.

 

Translation Hub:

For an overview of the SAP Translation Hub service and its features, refer to discovery center.

 

Step 1:

Create an instance for a service named SAP Translation Hub (technical name: document-translation) and generate its corresponding service key.

From the service key, retrieve ‘documenttranslation.url’, ‘uaa.clientid’, ‘uaa.clientsecret’ and ‘uaa.url’, which will be later used in integration scenario.

Step 2:

Explore document translation api at Business Accelerator Hub.

For the model parameter, two values are supported: default and llm. The default model uses SAP-trained Neural Machine Translation (NMT). For a list of supported languages, refer to the SAP Translation Hub documentation: Supported Languages

The llm falls under the generative AI category and can be used when a target language is not supported by the SAP-trained NMT model. For example, to translate text into Bengali.

 

Cloud Integration:

Step 1:

Create a security material of type ‘OAuth2 Client Credentials’.

Step 2:

Design the iFlow as illustrated below.

The Content Modifier is used to set the message header and body.  As only the translated text is required, the ‘Accept’ header is set to ‘application/octet-stream’.

The request body is configured as shown below:

Configure the HTTP receiver adapter to connect with document translation api as shown below.

Step 3:

Test the iFlow endpoint using an API testing tool. In the request payload, ‘orderId’ is a mandatory field as defined in the XML Validator step; therefore, any payload that does not include ‘orderId’ is routed to an exception subprocess.

Please note that all the data are fictitious.

 

Conclusion:

This blog post demonstrates a prototype for integrating Translation Hub with Cloud Integration.

entry.title: Getting ready for SAP Integration Suite at SAP Sapphire 2026!
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite - Sapphire 2026

 Hi Community, 

Excited to dive into what’s coming next for integration, AI, and real-time enterprise architecture at SAP Sapphire 2026. This year’s sessions clearly show how integration is evolving from a backend capability into a strategic enabler for AI-driven business. 

Don’t miss the opportunity to gain insights, learn from customer success stories, and experience how integration is becoming the backbone for AI innovation.  

Join us at SAP Sapphire 2026! SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference Orlando | SAP Sapphire Virtual | SAP Sapphire Madrid 

Save the dates and register today.  

🚨 Stay tuned for session highlights and updates throughout the event! Explore Integration Suite Catalogue here! 

SAP Integration Suite empowers organizations to connect applications, data, and processes across hybrid landscapes while enabling AI-driven innovation. From prebuilt integrations to event-driven architectures and governance for AI agents, this track highlights how integration is evolving into a strategic enabler for intelligent enterprises. Take a look at our SAP Integration Suite sessions and demos👇 

SAP Integration Suite at Sapphire 2026: Product Sessions

In these sessions, leading organizations share how they are leveraging SAP Integration Suite to solve real-world challenges. From enabling real-time commerce to scaling event-driven architectures and building AI-ready integration foundations, these stories provide practical insights into how integration drives business transformation across industries. 

BTP1445: Accelerate integrations with large volumes of adapters and prebuilt content 

BTP1446: Why organizations consolidate integration tools to prepare for AI 

BTP1447: Enterprise-ready AI integration: Securely connect and govern your AI agents 

BTP2100: Modernize from SAP Process Integration and SAP Process Orchestration 

SAP Integration Suite at Sapphire 2026: Customer Sessions

Leading organizations will share how they are leveraging SAP Integration Suite to solve real-world challenges. From enabling real-time commerce to scaling event-driven architectures and building AI-ready integration foundations, these stories provide practical insights into how integration drives business transformation across industries: 

BTP2350: FC Bayern Munich - Connecting fan data and systems with SAP Integration Suite 

BTP2138: Under Armour - Enabling real-time commerce with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) 

BTP2103: Siemens: Scaling real-time integration with event-driven design solutions 

BTP2637: Florida Crystals - Modernize B2B integration with SAP Integration Suite at scale 

BTP2638: PPG Industries - Building an AI-ready, clean core integration foundation

SAP Integration Suite at Sapphire 2026: Demo Stations

The demo stations offer a hands-on look at how SAP Integration Suite capabilities come to life in practice. These sessions will showcase how to modernize integrations for AI and implement governance for AI-driven scenarios, giving a closer view of tools and approaches that can be directly applied in enterprise landscapes. 

BTP2137: Modernize Integrations for AI 

BTP2138: Govern AI-Driven Integrations 

SAP Integration Suite at Sapphire 2026: ASUG Preer Insights

This session highlights the SAP Customer Influence site as a transparent, AI-powered platform for submitting, tracking, and prioritizing ideas that shape the future of SAP Integration Suite. It enables customers to share real-time feedback, influence product roadmaps, and gain visibility into how other organizations are driving integration-led transformation across their landscapes. 

BTP1606: Customer Influence and feedback: Influence SAP Integration Suite  

 

Explore these sessions and plan your agenda to get the most out of SAP Sapphire 2026. Don’t miss out!

entry.title: Integration capabilities highly rated in SAP BTP reviews
entry.content:
Only a few short months ago I posted a blog in celebration of SAP Business Technology Platform's (SAP BTP) performance in G2’s Winter 2026 awards for their Cloud Platform as a Service (PaaS) category. Now having quickly accelerated to their Spring 2026 awards, once again I am happy to report that SAP BTP featured prominently, achieving leader badges along with a leaders position in the G2 Enterprise Grid for CPaaS.  

 Connections count!

Reading just a handful of the most recent SAP BTP reviews on the G2 website reveals just how important robust yet flexible integration is to SAP customers. Among the most recent customer reviews, many reveal Integration as something for which they rate SAP BTP highly:

  • 18 April 2026 I use SAP Business Technology Platform for data integration, analytics, and app development. It connects multiple systems, reduces data silos, and streamlines workflows, providing real-time insights. Its scalability handles large data efficiently. 
  • 16 April 2026 SAP Business Technology Platform helps address the challenge of integrating, extending, and modernising SAP landscapes by offering a unified platform for integration, analytics, data management, and application development. For us, this means simpler system integration, better visibility through analytics, and faster innovation without disrupting our core SAP systems. 
  • 10 April 2026 By utilizing the SAP Integration Suite, we successfully automated the data flow between our core ERP and third-party tools. The biggest benefit is the dramatic reduction in manual IT maintenance, saving us countless hours each month. 
  • 16 March 2026 SAP Business Technology Platform helps us connect systems, manage data, and build extensions for SAP apps without changing the core system, keeping it stable. It speeds up development by allowing integration and new functions, ensuring easy modifications without risking the main setup. 
  • 12 February 2026  SAP technology primarily solves the problem of disconnected, inefficient, and siloed business processes within organizations. By acting as a "central nervous system," SAP integrates data across finance.         

And this is just a small sample of the reviews available on G2 – there are many more that speak about the benefits of SAP BTP not only for integration, but application development, automation and support for clean core development too.

Springing forward with new badges

Having secured our Spring 2026 badges from G2, what next for SAP BTP? Obviously, Summer 2026 badges are now in view, and with support from our fabulous customers we shall hope to achieve these next. But first for the SAP BTP team, along with others from SAP, will be a focus on our very own Sapphire 2026 events, due to take place in Orlando and Madrid next month. The SAP BTP team will be presenting at both of these prestigious events, so if you happen to be joining one or the other, please make sure to come and say hello to the team on the show floor. And if you’re unable to join either of these events in-person, then that’s no problem, instead please join us at the SAP Sapphire Virtual event.

 With that I’ll sign off for this time, but meanwhile don’t forget to go to the G2 website and read more reviews for the customer-viewpoint on SAP BTP!

Chris Grundy, Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: Triggering SAP CPI iflow through SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is for highlighting the basics of using SAP Build Process Automation and how it can be leveraged in various business scenarios. For demo purpose, I have created SAP CPI iflow which gets triggered by using a Automation job created in SAP BPA.

 

  1. Create CPI iflow test_iflow as below to capture data from Northwind opensource odata services with HTTPs sender adapter- 

 2. Activate the CPI iflow - 

 3. Create Destination in SAP BTP account using oauth credentials (client-id, client-secret created in BTP account) 

 

4. Add the created Destination in SAP Build->Control_Tower as below-  

 

 5. Create ‘Project’ ztestingprj_iflow under Connectors->Actions as below-  

 

 

 6.

a.  Create ‘Action’ zjoule_action under project ‘ztestingprj_iflow’ with URL [/http/ztest_joule] from endpoint generated from CPI iflow ‘test_iflow’. 

b. In the ‘Output’ tab, we will capture only some part of the response payload in the Body as the result.

c. In Destination, select the destination which was created in BTP 

d. Click Test. The Action triggers CPI iflow ‘test_iflow’ generating response [200 OK] with payload generated by CPI iflow.  

 

 7. Create ‘Process Automation’ project zProcess_prj under ‘Lobby’  

 

8. In Process Automation project created above; create Automation Artifact zauto1_sampleprocess as below- 

 

 9. In Automation Artifact created above, drag and drop the Action created in Step 6. 

 

 10. In ‘Project Properties’ create Environment Variable of type ‘destination’ as below and Save the  Automation Artifact- 

 11. To run the Automation in Desktop Agent create Automation Launcher zAutoLaunch1 as below- 

 

 12. In Automation Launcher created above drag and drop the Automation Artifact created in step 8. 

 13. Save the project. Release the required version and Deploy in ‘Public’ environment as below- 

 

 14. During Deployment, select the Destination created in BTP (step 3) in the Environment variable created in step 10.  

Click Deploy. 

 

 15. After successful Deployment the deployed version could be seen in the path as below- 

 16. Download Desktop Agent from SAP Development Tools as below and install in your system- 

 

 17. In Desktop Agent, click Add Tenant and register your SAP BPA account as below- 

 18. For Automation, set the Desktop Agent in ‘Unattended Mode’ as below- 

 

 19. After successful Tenant registration in Desktop Agent; we can see its entry in Agent Management under Control Tower path as below- 

 20. In ‘Triggers’ tab, create a trigger as per required schedule- 

 

 21. We can observe automatic triggering of the CPI iflow with the response payload- 

 22. In Monitoring->Automation Jobs; we can see the entries as per scheduled timings of trigger- 

 23. On Activating Traces, we can see the Traces showing response payloads coming from CPI iflow-

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP certified - Integration developer
entry.content:
You’ve probably worked with integrations that didn’t go as planned.  We’ve all been there.  APIs that don’t connect, flows that break, data that refuses to cooperate.

Now imagine walking into your next project with clarity.  Knowing exactly how to approach integration flows, APIs, and connectivity across SAP BTP.

That’s the goal of the Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified – Integration Developer.

In six live hours, we break down what you need for the C_CPI exam and, more importantly, what you need in real projects.  Plus, if you’ve already explored SAP Integration Suite, this is where everything starts to click.

The best part.  You don’t need to be an expert to start.  Just a solid foundation and the willingness to level up.

If certification is on your radar, this is a smart next move.
